You don’t need 10 years of experience to write senior-level Angular code you just need to know these 7 tricks that seniors never talk about.
The Hook
Every Angular developer hits that wall.
Your app works… but something feels off.
The code looks messy.
The change detection runs like a marathon.
And every time you open a component file, it screams for refactor.
Here’s the truth no one tells you
becoming a “senior” Angular dev isn’t about experience, it’s about awareness.
These 7 micro-patterns are what separate “good enough” Angular code from code that feels elegant, scalable, and smart.
Let’s dive in
1. Use readonly for Inputs You Never Mutate
You probably write this:
@Input() user!: User;But seniors write this:
@Input() readonly user!: User;Because they know the golden rule: never mutate parent data.
This small change makes your component safer and self-documenting.
